ccel.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

title: Concerning Imagination.

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Imagination is a faculty of the unreasoning part of the soul.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: It is through the organs of sense that it is brought into action, and it is spoken of as sensation.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And further, what is imagined and perceived is that which comes within the scope of the faculty of imagination and sensation.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For example, the sense of sight is the visual faculty itself, but the object of sight is that which comes within the scope of the sense of sight, such as a stone or any other such object.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Further, an imagination is an affection of the unreasoning part of the soul which is occasioned by some object acting upon the sensation.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But an appearance is an empty affection of the unreasoning part of the soul, not occasioned by any object acting upon the sensation.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Moreover the organ of imagination is the anterior ventricle of the brain.
